audit_no_functional_cc_dependency_remain.method: grep -rE 'exec.*claude\b|"claude"|claude --|claude code|@anthropic-ai/claude-code' across nexus + hexa-lang + anima tool/scripts dirs

matches_found.context: awk pattern matching `claude --effort` in process list
matches_found.purpose: OBSERVABILITY (detects CC processes for resource accounting); does not require CC presence
DEFENSE classification of process list; works whether CC is present or not

matches_found.context: literal banlist strings 'claude code cli hook' / 'claude code skill' / 'claude code init'
matches_found.purpose: DEFENSE against CC-related prompt injection patterns; the strings are PATTERNS to detect, not invocations

audit_no_functional_cc_dependency_remain.verdict: ZERO functional dependencies — every match is detection/observability/defense, not invocation. The codebase will operate identically with or without CC CLI installed.

remaining_cc_artifacts_intentionally_kep.rationale: These are session-state and operational config for the currently-running Claude Code session. They are NOT depended on by the codebase functionally — removing them would not affect any tool's behavior, but would terminate the in-flight CC session. Out of scope for this cycle (which targets codebase dependency, not session housekeeping). User can `rm -rf <repo>/.claude/` between sessions if desired without breaking any tool.

validation_post_cleanup.rollback_recipe: git revert c582f6da 80089030 262c8082; restore /Users/ghost/core/anima/CLAUDE.md from c582f6da^

what_this_does_not_do: does NOT remove .claude/ session directories (operational, not code dependency)
does NOT change CC-related defense/observability code (intentional detection of CC processes)
does NOT migrate the in-flight session off CC (this very session uses CC; the directive is forward-looking)
what_user_can_do_next_for_full_post_sess: deinstall Claude Code CLI from system if no longer needed for any session
